text: bundled with frida's gumjs runtime: Stop importing runtime bridges from Frida's GumJS runtime; they are no longer bundled and must be consumed separately via frida-tools 14.0.0 or frida-compile.

process.enumeratemodules({ onmatch(module) { ... }, oncomplete() { ... } }): Stop using the legacy callback-object pattern for enumeration APIs (e.g. Process.enumerateModules({onMatch:..., onComplete:...})); use the Sync-suffixed or new async equivalent instead.
process.enumeratemodules() with callbacks argument: Stop using callback-based Process.enumerateModules(); use the modern version that returns an array of Module objects.
Stop using Module.ensureInitialized(); it has been removed.
Stop using Module.findBaseAddress(); use Process.getModuleByName('name').base instead.
Stop using Module.getBaseAddress(); use Process.getModuleByName('name').base instead.
Stop using Module.findExportByName(); use Process.getModuleByName('name').getExportByName('export') instead.
Stop using Module.getExportByName(); use Process.getModuleByName('name').getExportByName('export') instead.
Stop using Module.findSymbolByName(); it has been removed.
Stop using Module.getSymbolByName(null, 'open'); use Module.getGlobalExportByName('open') instead.
Stop using Module.enumerateExports() static enumeration API; use Module.getExportByName() or equivalent per-module APIs instead.
Stop using Session.debugger API; use Script.debugger API instead.
usbmuxd_server_* envvars: Stop using USBMUXD_SERVER_* environment variables; they are no longer supported.
Stop using Python eggs for frida; use wheels instead.

memory.readu32(ptr), memory.writeu32(ptr, value): Stop using Memory.readU32(), Memory.writeU32() and similar static Memory read/write functions; use NativePointer instance methods like ptr().readU32() and ptr().writeU32() instead.
python 2 support: Stop using Python 2 with frida; Python 3 is now required.
setuptools as a runtime dependency: Stop relying on setuptools being installed at runtime; it has been removed as a runtime dependency.
python 2 compatibility code in wmi and ese modules: Stop using Python 2 compatibility patterns in WMI and ESE modules; the remaining Python 2 compatibility code has been removed.

text: Stop using Python 3.8 with impacket; Python 3.13 is now supported and 3.8 is dropped.
Stop using pkg_resources for version discovery; use importlib.metadata instead.
Stop depending on pyreadline; use pyreadline3 instead.
Stop relying on the dsinternals dependency; it has been removed from impacket.
ticketer.py now expects ticket duration in hours instead of days, so existing values will be interpreted as hours (e.g. 7 becomes 7 hours instead of 7 days).
anonymous logon was enabled in the smb server: Stop relying on anonymous logon in the SMB relay server; it is now disabled.

anonymous smb 2.x connections were encrypted: Anonymous SMB 2.x connections are no longer encrypted, which may break code relying on encrypted anonymous SMB 2.x connections.
Replace unmaintained PyCrypto with pycryptodome for cryptographic operations.
DPAPI_SYSTEM LSA Secret is now parsed and key contents are shown in secretsdump.py output.
smb3 packets are encrypted by default if target server supports smb >= 3: If target server supports SMB >= 3, packets are now encrypted by default instead of being sent in plaintext.

text: Stop using the old DCERPC runtime code; it has been removed and replaced with the NDR runtime overhaul.
single logger called 'impacket': Stop using per-module loggers; use the single 'impacket' logger instead.
Stop using the old RPC/NDR runtime; use the new one at impacket.dcerpc.v5 instead.
previous default port: Default SMB port is now 445 instead of any previous default.
`entry_raw_attributes` is populated instead: Stop using `raw_attributes` on EntryState; use `entry_raw_attributes` instead.
raw_dn was returned as a pyasn1 object: When using the pyasn1 decoder, `raw_dn` is now returned as bytes instead of a pyasn1 object.

text: strip parameter defaulted to true (or different value): The `strip` parameter in `utils.dn.parse_dn()` now defaults to `False` instead of `True`.

text: modify() did not mutate the passed object: The object passed to modify() is now mutated unexpectedly.

meta.symbol: modify() did not mutate the passed object

text: servers state is shared between connections using the same pool when single=true: ServerPool with single=True shares server state across connections, which may cause unexpected cross-connection side effects.

meta.symbol: servers state is shared between connections using the same pool when single=true

text: ServerState changed from namedtuple to class, breaking code that relied on namedtuple behaviour (e.g. immutability, tuple unpacking).
Connections in context manager no longer bind when auto_bind is set to AUTO_BIND_NONE, so code relying on automatic binding will break.

meta.symbol: connections in context manager no longer bind when auto_bind is set to auto_bind_none

text: accessing a missing entry's attribute raised an exception when searching: Accessing a missing entry's attribute now returns False instead of raising an exception.

text: formatters return the raw_value when unable to format: Formatters no longer raise exceptions but return the raw_value when unable to format.

text: Stop importing from 'async' module; import from 'asynchronous' module instead.
Stop using DEFAULT_ENCODING, use DEFAULT_CLIENT_ENCODING instead.
Stop using ADDITIONAL_ENCODINGS, use ADDITIONAL_SERVER_ENCONDINGS instead.
to_unicode() always tried client encoding: to_unicode() now has a from_server=False parameter; when True, it skips client encoding while decoding data from the server.
Stop using RESPONSE_DN_ENCODING, use ADDITIONAL_ENCODINGS instead.
Empty schema properties are now set to empty dict() instead of None, so code checking for None will break.
integer validator automatically converts valid string numbers to int: Integer validator now automatically converts valid string numbers to int instead of rejecting them.

meta.symbol: integer validator automatically converts valid string numbers to int

text: sha1 hashing algorithm: Updated hashing algorithm from SHA to SHA1.
Stop using to_bytes() and check_escape() from ldap3.utils.conv as they have been removed.

access via get_config_parameters() or get_config_parameter(): Config parameters CLASSES_EXCLUDED_FROM_CHECK, ATTRIBUTES_EXCLUDED_FROM_CHECK, UTF8_ENCODED_SYNTAXES, UTF8_ENCODED_TYPES are no longer directly accessible; use get_config_parameter() or get_config_parameters() instead.
no auto_escape (disabled): Automatic escaping of assertion values is now enabled by default; existing code that passed unescaped values may get double-escaped or behave differently.
dn sanitization was performed during bind: Sanitization of DN in bind operation was removed; servers that accepted non-standard DNs for Simple Bind will now receive them unsanitized.

meta.symbol: dn sanitization was performed during bind

text: Stop using the old signature of ldap3.abstract.Reader; use the new signature instead.
search_size_limit(), search_time_limit(), search_types_only: Stop calling search_size_limit(), search_time_limit(), and search_types_only on the Reader cursor; they have been removed.
Stop using LDAPTypeError; use LDAPAttributeError instead.
Stop using LDAPReaderError; use LDAPCursorError instead.
Stop using LDAPControlsError; use LDAPControlError instead.
Stop using LDAPChangesError; use LDAPChangeError instead.
Stop using compatibility constants; they have been removed from the ldap3 namespace.
Stop importing exceptions from the ldap3 namespace; import them from ldap3.core.exceptions instead.
Stop accessing RESULT_CODES from ldap3; use ldap3.core.results.RESULT_CODES instead.
Stop using 'check' parameter in connection.extend.novell.remove_members_from_groups(); use 'fix' instead.
unauthenticated authentication (bind without credentials): Unauthenticated authentication is now disabled per RFC 4513 section 5.1.2; binds without credentials will fail.
The compat package has been removed and is no longer available for import.
The exception SessionTerminatedByServer has been renamed to SessionTerminatedByServerError.
all_attributes does not send any attribute in the attribute list while searching: ALL_ATTRIBUTES no longer sends '*' as the attribute list during search; it sends an empty attribute list instead.

meta.symbol: all_attributes does not send any attribute in the attribute list while searching

text: connection.entries defaulted to none or similar: Connection.entries now defaults to an empty list instead of a falsy value like None.

meta.symbol: connection.entries defaulted to none or similar

text: Default server mode changed from IP_SYSTEM_DEFAULT to IP_V6_PREFERRED, which may cause IPv6 connection attempts before IPv4.
does not permit falsy values or none as default: Abstract Attribute no longer permits falsy values or None as default, which will raise errors if you previously passed such defaults.

meta.symbol: does not permit falsy values or none as default

text: response included in exception when raise_exceptions=true: Stop relying on the response attribute of exceptions raised with raise_exceptions=True; it has been removed to avoid long exception messages.

text: The SESSION_TERMINATED_BY_SERVER constant value changed from 0 to -2; any code comparing against 0 will break.
format_xxx variables in ldap3 namespace: Stop using FORMAT_xxx variables in the ldap3 namespace; they have been removed.
non-string constants: All constant values in ldap3 are now strings instead of their previous types; any code comparing constants with non-string types will break.
Stop using separe_rdn parameter in to_dn(); use separate_rdn instead.
Stop using convert_to_ldif(); it has been renamed to _convert_to_ldif() (now private/internal).
Stop using real_server machinery; messageId is now global and monotonic for the whole library.
Stop using checked_attributes; attributes are now returned formatted directly when schema is read and check_names=True.
Stop using connection.close(); use connection.unbind() instead.
check_names defaulted to false: check_names parameter in Connection now defaults to True, so attribute and class names will be validated against schema by default.
tlsv1 is no longer default: TLSv1 is no longer the default protocol for Tls connections; you must explicitly specify a protocol.
no exception raised: Receiving multiple extended responses to a single extended request now raises an exception (per RFC 4511).
Stop accessing server.schema.attribute_type by OID; use attribute name as the key instead.
Stop accessing server.schema.object_classes by OID; use class name as the key instead.
returns (response, result) tuple: Stop calling get_response() and expecting a single value; it now returns a tuple (response, result).
operation result is only in result attribute: Stop reading operation results from the response attribute; use the result attribute instead.

meta.symbol: operation result is only in result attribute

text: old signature without active/exhaust parameters: ServerPool constructor now requires or accepts active and exhaust parameters; update instantiation accordingly.
start_listen parameter existed: Stop passing start_listen parameter; it has been removed.
Stop importing from ldap3.abstraction; import from ldap3.abstract instead.
ldap3/connection.py, ldap3/server.py, ldap3/tls.py: Stop importing connection, server, or tls from the ldap3 root package; import from ldap3.core instead.
Stop using the forceBind parameter; it has been removed.

exceptions inherit from ldapexception (subclass of exception): Catch LDAPException instead of catching generic Exception or other LDAP exception types; all library exceptions now inherit from LDAPException.

text: attributes returned as tuple: Search response attributes changed from tuple to dict, so code accessing them by index will break.
authentication parameters on connection object: Authentication parameters moved from Server object to Connection object; code setting auth params on Server will silently be ignored.
Stop using the old constant names (e.g. AUTH_ANONYMOUS, SEARCH_SCOPE_BASE_OBJECT, STRATEGY_SYNC); use the new shorter names (e.g. ANONYMOUS, BASE, SYNC) instead.
Replace the command 'cme' with 'nxc' (also 'NetExec'/'netexec').
Replace the database tool 'cmedb' with 'nxcdb'.
Replace the home folder '~/.cme' with '~/.nxc'.
Replace the config file 'cme.conf' with 'nxc.conf'.
Replace the environment variable 'CME_PATH' with 'NXC_PATH'.
Replace the pip/pipx package 'crackmapexec' with 'netexec'.
reworked to enable throttling of authentications: The `--jitter` option has been reworked to throttle authentications instead of its previous behaviour.

meta.symbol: reworked to enable throttling of authentications

text: obfuscation and amsi bypasses were default: Obfuscation and AMSI bypasses in PowerShell execution are no longer enabled by default; they must be explicitly opted into.

text: old semantics of file download and upload flags: The semantics of NFS file download and upload flags have changed; check the new flag usage documentation before using them.

text: Stop using 'netexec' as the command; use 'nxc' instead.
Stop relying on the default ~/.nxc folder; set the NXC_PATH environment variable to configure the home folder.
default 100 threads (explicitly documented): The default number of concurrent threads is now 100.
timeout default is none (no timeout): The default timeout per thread is None (no timeout), which may cause threads to hang indefinitely.
jitter default is none (no delay): The default jitter between connections is None (no random delay), which may cause aggressive scanning.
Stop looking in '~/.cme/workspaces' and use '~/.nxc/workspaces' instead for database storage.

bloodhound community edition (ce) zip format: Stop using the legacy BloodHound collector zip; nxc now expects BloodHound CE zip format by default. To keep using the legacy collector, change the config setting.
pennyw0rth fork of impacket: The impacket dependency has been switched to the Pennyw0rth fork; existing code relying on the original impacket branch may break or behave differently.
fixed version of ldap source: The LDAP source has been switched to a fixed version; existing code depending on the old LDAP source may break.
The LDAP checker module has been removed; built-in checks for LDAP signing and channelBinding requirements are now shown in the host banner instead.
xdg base directory support: nxc now supports XDG Base Directory for its path/config; the old hardcoded path is no longer used, which may break scripts that rely on the old config location.
--multiple-hosts option (or equivalent): Stop using the multiple hosts option; it has been removed entirely.
Stop using the efsr_spray module; it has been removed and superseded by using EPM map on the EFS interface.
--debug and --verbose are now mutually exclusive: Using both --debug and --verbose together now causes an error; you must use only one.
sam/lsa dump via mssql no longer requires local admin: Stop requiring local admin for MSSQL SAM/LSA dump; the --sam/--lsa flags now work without local admin privileges.

meta.symbol: sam/lsa dump via mssql no longer requires local admin

text: smb protocol always performed admin check with sc_manager_all_access: Stop relying on the admin check always running; use --no-admin-check to disable the SC_MANAGER_ALL_ACCESS check that was previously mandatory.

text: winrm database stores fqdn instead of netbios name: Stop expecting NETBIOS names in the WinRM database; the stored hostname is now the FQDN.

text: Drop support for Python 3.8 and 3.9; you must use Python 3.10 or later.
smb in ldap protocol: Stop using SMB within the LDAP protocol; the LDAP protocol now uses plain LDAP only.
Stop using `ldapConnection`; rename it to `ldap_connection` in your code.
The default SMB connection protocol has been switched from SMBv1 to SMBv3.
The flag --cert-pem has been renamed to --pem-cert to match the PFX syntax convention.

default secretsdump technique (impacket-based, writes to disk): The default for --lsa and --sam is now the regsecretsdump technique; to use the old method you must explicitly specify it (e.g. --lsa secdump).

meta.symbol: default secretsdump technique (impacket-based, writes to disk)

text: Stop using the firefox module; use --dpapi instead which includes Firefox support.
restructured laps login to fix login issues: The LAPS login mechanism has been restructured, which may break existing LAPS login workflows.

meta.symbol: restructured laps login to fix login issues

text: --no-smb flag: Stop using --no-smb with MSSQL; SMB is no longer needed and the flag has been removed.
pyreadline library: Stop using pyreadline; it has been removed as it causes errors in nxcdb.
write without delete was not displayed as write access: Write without delete will now be displayed as write access, which may change permission interpretation in existing scripts.

meta.symbol: write without delete was not displayed as write access

text: WinRM no longer needs SMB to gather NTLM info; any workflow relying on SMB being used alongside WinRM will break.

meta.symbol: winrm no longer needs smb to gather ntlm info

text: Stop using MAQ.py; the module file has been renamed to maq.py (lowercase).
Stop using oscrypto; the library has been removed and swapped back to fortra/impacket.
Normal output that was previously logged at DEBUG level is now logged at INFO level, so it will appear in non-debug logging.
jitter option that randomly inserted delays: The old jitter option has been changed to a throttle authentications option that randomly inserts sleeps before authenticating.

text: ftp (original name): Stop using the class name 'Ftp' (capital F); the FTP class was accidentally renamed to 'Ftp' and has been fixed back to its original name.
Stop accessing port via self.args.port; port is now redirected to self.port directly.
gethost_addinfo using af_inet6/af_inet with try/except: The gethost_addinfo function now uses AF_UNSPEC instead of trying AF_INET6 then AF_INET, and IPv4 is preferred when target is dual stack.
Stop using the --opsec flag; it has been removed entirely.
pywerview dependency: Stop relying on pywerview being available as a dependency; it has been removed.
ssh_agent enabled by default: SSH agent usage is now disabled; code relying on ssh_agent being available will break.
Replace the template block key 'requests:' with 'http:' for HTTP protocol definitions.
Replace the template block key 'network:' with 'tcp:' for TCP/network protocol definitions.
Stop using deprecated template paths; update all template path references before v2.9.5 or they will fail to load.
old protocol syntax (e.g. 'requests:' instead of 'http:'): Stop using old protocol syntax in templates; update to new syntax before v2.9.5 or templates will fail to load.

explicit `flow:` field using javascript (ecmascript 5.1 via goja) to orchestrate/conditionally execute requests: Stop relying on implicit sequential request execution; you must now use the explicit `flow:` field to orchestrate request execution order.

text: Stop using `-fuzz`; use `-dast` instead.
-cup / -cloud-upload: Stop using -cloud-upload (-cup); use -dashboard (-pd) instead to upload scan results to the cloud dashboard.
-rlm / -rate-limit-minute: Stop using `-rlm` or `-rate-limit-minute`; use `-rl` or `-rate-limit` instead.

Stop using -include-rr (deprecated); use -omit-raw instead, and note the default behaviour has inverted (include-rr defaulted true, omit-raw defaults to false meaning request/response are now included by default).
autofallback to ztls enabled by default: Stop using `-ztls` flag; ztls autofallback is now enabled by default.

text: redirects: true (per-request): The `redirects` field is now defined per template, not per request block.

exclude-ports field overwrites the default reserved ports list entirely: Setting exclude-ports in a network template completely overwrites the default reserved ports list, so any port not in exclude-ports will be treated as non-reserved and will use the user-supplied port.

meta.symbol: exclude-ports field overwrites the default reserved ports list entirely

text: Starting from Nuclei v3.1.0, the port field supports comma-separated values, and Nuclei will check which ports are open from the list and run the template only on open ports.

raw / body / all: Stop using 'raw', 'body', or 'all' as field names in network protocol templates; use 'request' for the network request and 'data' for the final data read from the network socket instead.
Protocol execution functions (e.g., `ssl()`, `http()`) now require an index or ID argument to execute a specific request; calling them without arguments may no longer work or may behave differently.

text: filters are applied on installed path of templates and can be customized with manual template path input: Filters now require explicit template path (-t) when used with -tags; they no longer automatically scan all installed templates unless -t is also provided.

meta.symbol: filters are applied on installed path of templates and can be customized with manual template path input

text: -ai flag for ai-powered template generation: The `-ai` flag is now used to generate templates via AI, requires internet and a ProjectDiscovery API key, and is limited to 100 queries per day.
Stop using the separate -metrics port; metrics are now served on the -stats port at localhost:9092/metrics when -stats is enabled.
users could modify .nuclei-ignore file to customize ignore list: Stop modifying .nuclei-ignore directly; use the nuclei configuration file to overwrite the ignore list.

meta.symbol: users could modify .nuclei-ignore file to customize ignore list

text: previous config file location (pre-v2.3.2): The default config file path is now $HOME/.config/nuclei/config.yaml.
PDCP API key must now be set via the PDCP_API_KEY environment variable or the credentials file at $HOME/.pdcp/credentials.yaml instead of an interactive prompt.

`name` is the dropdown value, `id` is the id value of the dropdown, `freeform` is for text entry: Stop using `name` as the dropdown ID; `name` is now the dropdown display value, `id` is the dropdown ID, and `freeform` is for free-text custom fields.

text: using `account-id` and `token` for jira cloud, or `account-id` as username and `token` as password for on-prem: Stop using `username`/`password` fields; use `account-id` (username for on-prem) and `token` (password for on-prem) instead.

text: using `closed_status` variable or a field named `closed-status`: Stop using `CLOSED_STATUS`; use the `status-not` variable in the Jira template file to set the closed status for duplicate detection.

text: `severity-as-label` is optional; user custom fields for jira cloud are recommended instead: Stop relying solely on `severity-as-label`; use Jira Cloud custom fields for severity instead, as `severity-as-label` is now optional.

text: fuzzing/dos templates are excluded from default scan and require -itags flag: Fuzzing and DoS templates are excluded from the default scan and can only be executed when explicitly invoked using the -itags option.

meta.symbol: fuzzing/dos templates are excluded from default scan and require -itags flag

text: before v3.1.4, multi-protocol templates with `flow` only returned one result: From v3.1.4, nuclei prints all events/results in multi-protocol/flow templates instead of only one result, and template writers must use `internal: true` on matchers to suppress intermediate results.

text: The export hash (exphash) algorithm changed from SHA-256 to MD5 to match imphash.
IOError has been replaced with OSError; code catching IOError will no longer catch exceptions from pefile.
Stop using pefile on Python 2.7; it is no longer supported and legacy Python 2 code has been removed.

text: bytes (or similar mutable type): Stop relying on __data__ being immutable; it is now a bytearray and may be mutated in place.
Stop using bdist_rpm configuration; it has been removed.
20221023 storage format: Stop using .plaso storage files in the 20221023 format; they cannot be read and must be regenerated.
renamed sqlite storage reader/writer class names: Stop importing the old SQLite storage reader/writer class names; use the renamed versions.

meta.symbol: renamed sqlite storage reader/writer class names

text: winlnk no longer generates duplicate DLT events; code relying on duplicate DLT events will break.

meta.symbol: winlnk no longer generates duplicate dlt events

text: Stop using Python 3.7; plaso now requires Python 3.8+.
data files moved into python module: Data files were moved into the Python module; code or scripts referencing the old external data file paths will break.

text: sessionstart, sessionconfiguration, sessioncompletion attribute container classes: Stop using SessionStart, SessionConfiguration, and SessionCompletion from the storage/session API; they have been removed.

text: Stop using the event `parser` attribute; use `parser_chain` instead. The backwards-compatibility shim has been removed.
Stop passing --process_archives to log2timeline.py; it has been removed and invocations will error.
text-based (line-based) filter files: Stop using text-based filter files; filtering now requires YAML-based artifact filter files.
Stop referencing the plist plugin as `macosx_install_history`; use `macos_install_history` instead. The old name no longer matches.
Stop running log2timeline.py, psort.py, pinfo.py, psteal.py, image_export.py as standalone scripts; use the installed console_scripts entry points instead.

text: Stop using pwnlib.util.iters.lookahead; it has been removed entirely.
python 2.7 support: Stop using Python 2.7 or Python <3.10; pwntools now requires Python 3.10 or newer.
sign parameter accepted as positional argument: Stop passing `sign` as a positional argument to packing utilities; it now requires keyword syntax.

text: -e flag deprecated in pwn asm: Stop using the `-e` flag in `pwn asm`; it is deprecated.
Stop using 'keepends' argument in tube.recvline* methods; use 'drop' argument instead.
direct commandline scripts invocation (e.g. `pwn asm` as a script): Stop invoking pwntools scripts directly from the command line; use the 'pwn' command with subcommands instead.

asm() emitted p2align 2 headers: asm() no longer emits p2align 2 headers for x86-32, x86-64, and mips architectures, which changes instruction length when patching binaries.
buffer inherited from some other class: Buffer's parent class changed to object, which may break code relying on Buffer's previous inheritance hierarchy.

text: sys.exc_type attribute: PwnlibException no longer has sys.exc_type; use self.message and sys.exc_info() instead.
unconditional imports in pwnlib: pwnlib no longer does unconditional imports; several modules are now deferred to save startup time, which may break code relying on side effects of those imports.
pwnlib.adb module: Stop using pwnlib.adb; Android tests are disabled and it is no longer supported in CI.
eval from cli: Stop using the eval CLI command; it has been removed.
Stop relying on pwn.toplevel.__all__; it has been removed.
gdb not terminated by default when using run_in_new_terminal: run_in_new_terminal now terminates gdb by default; existing code relying on gdb staying open will break.

meta.symbol: gdb not terminated by default when using run_in_new_terminal

meta.symbol: process() only looked at system path for the executable

text: tubes used hardcoded newline (\n): Tubes now use `context.newline` instead of a hardcoded newline character by default.
docker images used python2 by default: Docker images (`pwntools/pwntools:stable` etc) now use Python3 by default instead of Python2.

meta.symbol: docker images used python2 by default

text: scripts had python2 shebangs: Python2 shebangs have been removed from scripts where appropriate.
tubes.ssh.process() required python 2 installed on remote: Stop relying on Python 2 being installed on the remote host when using tubes.ssh.process(); it now only requires Python (any version).

meta.symbol: tubes.ssh.process() required python 2 installed on remote

meta.symbol: flat(..., filler=) with str values or python2 bytes may have worked differently

text: flat() may have mis-ordered fields: Stop relying on flat() producing mis-ordered fields; the fix corrects field ordering which may change output.
Stop using six versions below 1.12.0 with pwntools; you must upgrade six to v1.12.0 or higher.
flat() and fit() were separate functions: flat() and fit() are now the same function; code relying on them being distinct may silently change behaviour.

meta.symbol: flat() and fit() were separate functions

text: intervaltree 3.xx was allowed: IntervalTree 3.xx is disallowed; only IntervalTree 2.xx is supported.
Stop using the old undocumented mechanism for changing logging colors; use the pwnlib.config module with settings in ~/.pwn.conf instead.
Stop using process.STDOUT, process.PIPE, process.PTY; use the global STDOUT, PIPE, PTY constants instead.

text: ROP class now respects context.bytes instead of using the hard-coded value of 4.

text: sh_string emits strings that are no longer copy-paste compatible: Stop relying on sh_string output being copy-paste compatible; the new output embeds single-quoted non-printable literals and works only with tubes and subprocess modules.

text: shellcraft.sh did not provide argv[0] or set argc==1: shellcraft.sh now always provides argv[0] and sets argc==1, which may break systems that relied on the old behaviour (e.g. minimal shells that previously worked with argc==0).

text: stdout and stderr are now ptys by default: Stop assuming process stdout and stderr are pipes; they are now PTYs by default, which changes buffering and read behaviour.

meta.symbol: stdout and stderr are now ptys by default

text: Stop using ssh.run() for running commands over SSH; use ssh.process() instead.
Stop using listen_remote(); use listen() instead.
Stop using connect_remote(); use remote() instead.
bundled binutils were included: Stop relying on bundled binutils; you must build them yourself as documented.
Stop relying on the Bowser listener; it has been removed from Responder.
machine accounts were dumped: Stop relying on machine account dumps; they have been removed because they are not crackable.
-r option existed: Stop using the -r flag; it has been removed and all references to it removed from help.

text: fingerprint.py module: Stop using fingerprint.py; it has been removed from the repository.
Stop using analyze mode on DNS; it has been removed because ARP is required to get queries.
Stop using the old standalone DHCP script; it has been replaced by the built-in DHCP Responder module.
static default certificates bundled with responder: Stop relying on static default certificates; they have been removed and certificates are now auto-generated on startup.

text: findsmb2uptime.py script: Stop using FindSMB2UPTime.py; RunFinger already provides this information.

bindshell executable file: Stop using the BindShell executable; it has been removed from the repository.
old ldap hash parsing algorithm: The complete LDAP hash parsing algorithm was changed to fix an NTLMv2 bug; existing NTLMv2 hashes parsed from LDAP may no longer be extracted correctly.
threadingmixin (concurrent request handling in multirelay): Stop using ThreadingMixIn; MultiRelay now processes one request at a time and queues subsequent requests.
smbv1 only (default): SMBv2 is now enabled by default; existing setups that relied on SMBv1-only behaviour may now negotiate SMBv2.
Replace calls to IsOSX with utils.IsOsX.
The config dump is no longer written to Responder-Session.log; a new separate log file is created with host network config and config dump.
-r on / -r off style options: Stop using old style options like '-r On'; use '-r' directly instead.

text: wpad was on by default: WPAD is now disabled by default; use -w On to enable it.

could bind to a specific interface on osx: Bind to interface support is removed for OsX; Responder on OsX can only listen on all interfaces.

Stop using uc_context_free(), use uc_free() instead.
